perturbation.original_text: The term "Client Content" means all text, words, names, likenesses, trademarks, logos, artwork, graphics, video, audio, HTML, JAVA or other coding, domain names, image maps, links, software applications, or other content that appear on, or are provided to TrueLink by or on behalf of Client for uploading to or downloading from, the Web Site.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Client Content" means all text, words, names, likenesses, trademarks, logos, artwork, graphics, video, audio, HTML, JAVA or other coding, domain names, image maps, links, software applications, or other data that appear on, or are provided to TrueLink by or on behalf of BNC Mortgage, Inc. for uploading to or downloading from, the Web Site.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Client Content' as content provided by 'Client'. The modified text, without defining 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' interchangeably with 'Client,' redefines 'Client Content' as that which is provided by 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.'. This creates uncertainty about whose content is actually being referred to as 'Client Content' throughout the contract, especially when 'Client' and 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' are mentioned separately elsewhere in the document. The In-Text contradiction arises from making it unclear whether Client is the same legal entity as BNC Mortgage, Inc.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Client represents and warrants: (a) Client is the owner, valid licensee, or authorized user of the Client Content, (b) to Client's actual knowledge the use of the Client Content shall not infringe the copyright, trade secret, trademark or other proprietary or intellectual property right of any third party, or constitute a definition, invasion of privacy, or violation of any right of publicity or other third party right, (c) the Client Content complies in all material respects with applicable federal and state laws regarding posting or transmitting data which is threatening, obscene, indecent, defamatory or in violation of report control was, and (d) to Client's actual knowledge Client Content shall be free at the time provided to TrueLink from viruses, worms, Trojan horses, and any other malicious code.
perturbation.justification: The modified text redefines 'Client Content' as content provided by 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' without explicitly stating that 'Client' and 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' are the same entity. The contradicted text places specific obligations and representations on 'Client' regarding 'Client Content,' such as ownership and compliance with laws. The change creates ambiguity as to whether these obligations now apply to 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' or if 'Client' still has separate responsibilities, potentially leading to unenforceability of the original warranties. This creates a direct and impactful contradiction because the contract now lacks clarity on who is responsible for the content and its compliance, which is central to the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Client will pay TrueLink's fees for requested Hosting Services and Support Services pursuant to the schedule of charges set forth on Exhibits "A" and "B" attached hereto, (but not less than $____ per month for the Hosting Services and $____ per month for Support Services).
perturbation.changed_text: BNC Mortgage, Inc. will pay TrueLink's fees for requested Hosting Services and Support Services pursuant to the schedule of charges set forth on Exhibits "A" and "B" attached hereto, (but not less than $____ per month for the Hosting Services and $____ per month for Support Services).
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that 'Client' will pay the fees. The modified text specifies that 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' will pay. This introduces a contradiction as 'Client' and 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' are not explicitly defined as the same entity, creating uncertainty about who is ultimately responsible for payment. The In-Text contradiction arises when the contract discusses the obligation of 'Client' elsewhere without clarifying that this refers to 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' in this instance. Furthermore, changing who makes the payments raises question in case of Disputes and Documentation (section 6), where it is specified for only the 'Client'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Client will be provided upon request with documentation supporting the amount charged (other than the minimum monthly fees listed in Section 4) and will be entitled to contest any charge (other than the minimum monthly fees listed in Section 4), provided that Client timely pays all contested amounts. TrueLink agrees that the costs for any Hosting Services will not increase for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement and that rates charged for Hosting Services will not exceed that charged by TrueLink to any other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Client' with 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' as the entity responsible for payments. The contradicted text refers to 'Client' when discussing documentation and contesting charges. Since 'Client' and 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' are not explicitly defined as the same entity, there's a direct contradiction: it's unclear whether BNC Mortgage, Inc. (the new payer) has the same rights to documentation and dispute resolution as the original 'Client' as referred to in the contradicted text. This creates uncertainty and impacts the enforceability of the documentation and dispute resolution clauses, as it is unclear if the original obligation is still valid to the 'Client' as referenced in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall renew automatically thereafter for successive one year periods until terminated pursuant to Section 12 herein or unless either Client or TrueLink deliver to the other written notice of intent not to renew no later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of said year.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall renew automatically thereafter for successive one year periods until terminated pursuant to Section 12 herein or unless either BNC Mortgage, Inc. or TrueLink deliver to the other written notice of intent not to renew no later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of said year.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that 'Client' can deliver a notice of intent not to renew. The modified text specifies that 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' can deliver the notice. This introduces a contradiction because it creates uncertainty as to who is authorized to make decisions regarding the renewal of the agreement, especially considering that 'Client' and 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' are not established as the same entity. The In-Text contradiction occurs due to the fact that several other parts of the contract specifies for 'Client' and now in this section another entity is being introduced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will take effect on the Effective Date and remain in effect for a period of 1 year; provided, that Sections 2 and 3 level shall terminate promptly upon (a) any action or omission by Client or any of its customers which constitutes a breach of or default by TrueLink under any System Agreement or Credit Repository Agreement, which breach or default has either not been cured or cannot be cured within the applicable cure period and the consequences of which is that TrueLink will lose material rights it had pursuant to said Agreements or (b) any violations or breach by Client of Sections 8a - 8c. This Agreement shall renew automatically thereafter for successive one year periods until terminated pursuant to Section 12 herein or unless either BNC Mortgage, Inc. or TrueLink deliver to the other written notice of intent not to renew no later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of said year. If Client decides to discontinue its Hosting Service, Client is responsible for arranging for a new name server within 30 days. In case of discontinued Service, Client shall retain full ownership of all domains associated with Client and Client Content. If Client decides to discontinue Hosting Service, Client is responsible for arranging for a new hosting environment within 60 days.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'Client' as the entity able to provide notice of intent not to renew. The changed text replaces 'Client' with 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text repeatedly refers to 'Client' and their responsibilities, such as discontinuing hosting service and arranging for a new name server. Now, the contract uses 'BNC Mortgage, Inc.' for renewal notices but continues to reference 'Client' for other key obligations, introducing uncertainty and potential conflict regarding who is responsible for what. This misalignment of terminology leads to an in-text contradiction.
